rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical synthesis of Z-selective 2-alkylidene-tetrahydrofurans via sequential paired electrolysis coupling activated organochlorides (trichloro esters/amides) with olefins, enabled by halogen-bond (XB) activation using dtbbpy as the XB acceptor
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of the noncovalent XB complex (dtbbpy···Cl-CCl2-) lowers the SET barrier, generating a C-centered radical after C-Cl fragmentation; the resulting alkyl radical (after addition to olefin) is then oxidized at the anode to a cation (radical-polar crossover)

key_intermediates.name: Noncovalent halogen-bond complex IV between dtbbpy (N lone pair) and the C-Cl bond of trichloro ester/amide I
α,α-Dichloro carbon radical V generated after cathodic SET and Cl- loss from complex IV
Alkyl radical VI from addition of V to alkene II
Cation VII formed by anodic oxidation of radical VI (radical-polar crossover)
2-Alkylidene-tetrahydrofuran III formed by keto-enol tautomerization and intramolecular O-cyclization onto cation VII

electron_flow: dtbbpy halogen-bonds to a C-Cl of the trichloro substrate forming complex IV, which is cathodically reduced and fragments to Cl- and α,α-dichloro radical V; V adds to the alkene to give radical VI, which migrates to the anode and is oxidized to cation VII; the pendant carbonyl tautomerizes to an enol and cyclizes intramolecularly to deliver Z-2-alkylidene-tetrahydrofuran III.
rate_determining_step: Cathodic SET reduction of the C-Cl bond, whose activation barrier is lowered by XB complexation with dtbbpy (reduction potential shifted positively by 220 mV)

probe_reagents.name: dtbbpy (4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine) as XB acceptor
probe_reagents.observation: CV showed +220 mV positive shift of the C-Cl reduction potential upon adding dtbbpy, evidencing XB-mediated activation

probe_reagents.name: UV-vis titration of dtbbpy with trichloro substrate
probe_reagents.observation: UV-vis changes consistent with formation of noncovalent XB complex IV

probe_reagents.name: NiCl2(dtbbpy) control catalyst
probe_reagents.observation: Use of 20 mol% NiCl2(dtbbpy) with Pt cathode gave only 9% of 3, excluding Ni-catalyzed C-Cl activation from Ni-leached species

probe_reagents.name: Less polar solvent screen (PhCN, 1,2-DCE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace or much lower yields in solvents known to disrupt noncovalent interactions, supporting XB being essential

probe_reagents.name: Alternative XB-acceptor analogs (bpy, 1,10-phen derivatives, 1,4-dicyanobenzene, tetrathiafulvalene)
probe_reagents.observation: All gave decreased or trace yield, highlighting unique reactivity of dtbbpy as XB acceptor

notes: Alternative mechanism considered and excluded: in situ formation of a Ni-dtbbpy complex from the nickel foam cathode acting as an inner-sphere catalyst for C-Cl activation; control with NiCl2(dtbbpy)/Pt gave only 9% product, ruling this out. Key challenges noted by the authors: (1) low stability of weakly bound XB complex IV (XB-donor strength F<<Cl<<Br<I makes Cl weak); (2) potential 2e- overreduction of electron-deficient radicals V and VI at the cathode; (3) lifetime/diffusion of V and VI must be sufficient for sequential paired electrolysis (anode trip for radical-polar crossover). Exclusive Z-selectivity of the alkylidene tetrahydrofuran is a notable stereochemical outcome but its origin is not mechanistically dissected on these pages.
